title: APPENDIX — Quick-reference summary (not for reading aloud)

lines.text: APPENDIX — Quick-reference summary (not for reading aloud)
Core idea: cancer is a cell that has forgotten the rules of multicellular cooperation.
It accumulates capabilities (hallmarks) through driver mutations, then evolves as a Darwinian population inside the patient under any selection pressure (immune, drug, environmental).

Hallmarks of Cancer (Hanahan & Weinberg): sustained proliferation; evading growth suppressors; resisting apoptosis; replicative immortality; angiogenesis; invasion/metastasis; reprogrammed metabolism (Warburg); immune evasion; enabled by genome instability and tumor-promoting inflammation.
Two gene classes — the central asymmetry: Oncogenes (gas pedals stuck on, dominant, druggable): RAS, MYC, EGFR, BCR-ABL, HER2, BRAF, ALK, KIT.
Tumor suppressors (broken brakes, recessive — Knudson two-hit, mostly not directly druggable): TP53 (~50% of cancers), RB, BRCA1/2 (synthetic lethality with PARP inhibitors), PTEN, APC.
Cell cycle: G1 → S → G2 → M, gated by CDK-cyclin foremen at checkpoints.
Cyclin D–CDK4/6 phosphorylate RB, release E2F, commit to S.
CDK4/6 inhibitors (palbociclib, ribociclib, abemaciclib) in HR+ breast cancer (requires intact RB).
Apoptosis: BCL2 family standoff at mitochondrion — pro-survival (BCL2) vs effectors (BAX, BAK) vs BH3-only sensors.
BCL2 overexpression in follicular lymphoma via t(14;18).
Venetoclax = BH3 mimetic, treats CLL/AML; risk of tumor lysis syndrome.
Metastasis: angiogenesis (VEGF → bevacizumab); EMT (loss of E-cadherin, SNAIL/TWIST/ZEB); seed-and-soil (Paget 1889); pre-metastatic niche.
Tumor microenvironment: CAFs (desmoplasia), TAMs (M2), MDSCs, Tregs, hypoxia (HIF-1α), Warburg/lactate acidosis.
Immune evasion via PD-L1/PD-1, CTLA-4 checkpoints.
Cancer genomics: TCGA established molecular taxonomy.
Drivers vs passengers.
Mutational signatures (COSMIC) = fingerprints of UV, tobacco, age, BRCA, MMR, APOBEC.
MSI-high / TMB-high → checkpoint-inhibitor responsive (tissue-agnostic approval).
Targeted therapy landmark: imatinib for BCR-ABL in CML (2001) — the proof that single-target small molecules work.
Followed by EGFR (gefitinib, erlotinib, osimertinib), HER2 (trastuzumab, T-DM1, T-DXd), BRAF V600E (vemurafenib + MEK inhibitor), ALK (crizotinib, alectinib), KRAS G12C (sotorasib).
Immunotherapy: Checkpoint inhibitors: anti-CTLA-4 (ipilimumab), anti-PD-1 (pembrolizumab, nivolumab), anti-PD-L1 (atezolizumab).
Allison & Honjo Nobel 2018.
irAEs from de-restrained autoimmunity.
CAR-T: CD19 in B-cell malignancies, BCMA in myeloma.
Made with lentiviral vectors.
Risks: CRS (manage with tocilizumab), ICANS.
Prevention & screening: tobacco = #1 modifiable risk.
Vaccines: HPV (cervical and others), HBV (liver).
Screening: Pap/HPV, mammography, colonoscopy, low-dose chest CT in smokers, controversial PSA.
Classical pillars (still cure most cancers that get cured): Surgery: best for localized disease.
Radiation: DNA damage; fractionation, conformal techniques, abscopal effect with immunotherapy.

Chemotherapy classes: alkylators (cyclophosphamide, platinums), antimetabolites (methotrexate, 5-FU, gemcitabine), topoisomerase inhibitors (anthracyclines, irinotecan), microtubule poisons (vincas, taxanes).
Regimens: R-CHOP, FOLFIRINOX, ABVD, BEP.
Resistance — Darwinian evolution in real time: on-target (T315I in BCR-ABL, T790M in EGFR), bypass (MET amplification), lineage plasticity (SCLC transformation), microenvironmental.
Adaptive therapy = exploiting competition between sensitive and resistant clones.
Liquid biopsy (ctDNA): genotyping, response monitoring, early resistance detection, minimal residual disease, multi-cancer early detection (Galleri).
AI/computational: whole-slide pathology AI, radiology AI, AlphaFold and protein structure for drug discovery, multi-omics integration.

Frontier: personalized mRNA neoantigen vaccines + checkpoint blockade; base/prime editing for cell therapies; patient-derived organoids for drug sensitivity testing; PROTACs and molecular glues for "undruggable" targets; CRISPR synthetic-lethality screens; microbiome modulation of immunotherapy response.

Two organizing principles: (1) every hallmark is a logically necessary capability and therefore a therapeutic target — knowing how cancer cells differ from normal predicts the drug; (2) every therapy applies a selection pressure and the cancer evolves under it — durable benefit requires managing the evolutionary dynamics, not just hitting the target once.
